quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 13 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 13 Quiz 12: Advanced Systems & Inequalities — Heavy Hitters (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 13: Advanced Systems & Inequalities — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Solve the system: $2x - y + 3z = 10$, $x + 2y - z = -3$, $3x - y + 2z = 11$.
questions.explanation: From eq 1: $y = 2x+3z-10$. Substitute into eqs 2 and 3. Get $x+z = 17/5$ and $x-z = 1$. Solve: $x = 11/5$, $z = 6/5$, $y = -2$.

questions.question: Find the interior vertex (not on an axis) of the region: $x \ge 0$, $y \ge 0$, $x + 2y \le 8$, $3x + y \le 9$.
questions.explanation: Intersection of $x+2y=8$ and $3x+y=9$: from second $y=9-3x$, substitute → $x+2(9-3x)=8$ → $x=2$, $y=3$. Vertex $(2,3)$.

questions.question: Which point satisfies $y \ge x^2 - 4$ and $y < -x + 2$?
questions.explanation: $(0,0)$: $0 \ge -4$ ✓; $0 < 2$ ✓. Other options fail at least one inequality.

questions.question: Find all vertices of: $x \ge 0$, $y \ge 0$, $x + y \le 5$, $2x + y \le 8$, $x + 2y \le 8$.
questions.explanation: Intersect each pair of boundaries, test against remaining constraints. Five vertices: $(0,0)$, $(4,0)$, $(3,2)$, $(2,3)$, $(0,4)$.

questions.question: A diet needs at least $20$g protein, $15$g fiber. Food X: $4$g protein, $3$g fiber, costs $\$2$. Food Y: $2$g protein, $5$g fiber, costs $\$3$. Minimize cost.
questions.explanation: Constraints: $4x+2y \ge 20$, $3x+5y \ge 15$. Vertices: $(5,0)$ cost $\$10$; $(0,10)$ cost $\$30$. Minimum at $(5,0)$.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 13: 3×3 systems with fractions, nonlinear systems with circles, finding interior vertices, nonlinear inequalities, multi-constraint LP, optimization, absolute value equations and inequalities, and cost minimization.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) 3×3 systems: substitute or eliminate systematically, (2) Circle-line systems yield quadratics, (3) LP: evaluate the objective at every feasible vertex, (4) $|ax+b| < c$ is a compound inequality; $|ax+b| > c$ splits into two.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Solving systems with fractions — keep them as fractions, (2) Testing vertices by finding all boundary intersections then checking constraints, (3) Absolute value: less than → between; greater than → outside, (4) For LP word problems, extract constraints carefully from the problem statement.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 13 fundamentals: (1) 3×3 systems: use substitution or elimination to reduce to 2×2, (2) Nonlinear systems: substitute and solve the resulting equation, (3) LP: graph constraints, find vertices, evaluate objective at each, (4) $|ax+b| = c$ → $ax+b = \pm c$; $|ax+b| < c$ → $-c < ax+b < c$; $|ax+b| > c$ → $ax+b > c$ or $ax+b < -c$.
